title: mseg-tester results
description: Visualizes internal/report.PushInflux's two measurements: mseg_tester_result (one line per segment run: pass, updated, version) and mseg_tester_check (one line per individual check within that run: pass, detail). Points at whatever InfluxDB v2 bucket config.yaml's report.influx is configured to write into (examples/config.yaml default: org "homeassistant", bucket "mseg-tester").

panels.title: Current status per segment
panels.description: Latest mseg_tester_result.pass per segment -- PASS/FAIL, colored. One series per segment tag.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: -7d)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "mseg_tester_result")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "pass")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => contains(value: r.segment, set: ${segment:json}))
  |> group(columns: ["segment"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "segment"])

panels.title: Latest run per segment (version / updated / age)
panels.description: Most recent mseg_tester_result row per segment, pivoted so pass/updated/version are columns.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: -30d)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "mseg_tester_result")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => contains(value: r.segment, set: ${segment:json}))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> group(columns: ["segment"])
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"], desc: true)
  |> limit(n: 1)
  |> group()
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "segment", "pass", "updated", "version"])
  |> sort(columns: ["segment"])

panels.title: Segment pass/fail over time
panels.description: One row per segment. Green = whole batch of checks passed that cycle, red = at least one check failed (state.Result.Pass()).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "mseg_tester_result")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "pass")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => contains(value: r.segment, set: ${segment:json}))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "segment"])

panels.title: Latest result for every check
panels.description: Most recent mseg_tester_check row per (segment, check) -- pass + detail, e.g. failure reason from checks.go (dns timeout, wrong geo, etc.).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: -30d)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "mseg_tester_check")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => contains(value: r.segment, set: ${segment:json}))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> group(columns: ["segment", "check"])
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"], desc: true)
  |> limit(n: 1)
  |> group()
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "segment", "check", "pass", "detail"])
  |> sort(columns: ["segment", "check"])

panels.title: Per-check pass/fail history
panels.description: Every individual check (dhcp, dhcp6, dns-local:*, dns-remote:*, reverse, reverse6, geo, routing, routing6, selfupdate) as its own row, segment prefixed. Use the segment variable above to narrow this down -- it gets crowded with all segments' checks at once.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "mseg_tester_check")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "pass")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => contains(value: r.segment, set: ${segment:json}))
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with series: r.segment + " / " + r.check }))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "series"])

panels.title: Pass rate per segment (1h windows)
panels.description: Share of check batches that fully passed, aggregated hourly -- a slow-degradation signal that a single PASS/FAIL state doesn't show (e.g. a check that's flaky rather than fully broken).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "mseg_tester_result")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "pass")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => contains(value: r.segment, set: ${segment:json}))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value * 100.0 }))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "segment"])
